summary: MediGuard AI – iteration-2 testing of 3 new features (caregiver magic-link, PDF report export, Gemini pill visual check) plus full regression. 9/9 new backend tests pass (caregiver invite create/list/revoke + expired→410, public read-only with no auth, sensitive-field sanitisation, PDF export with %PDF magic + content-disposition + 404 for unknown, pill check schema + real-Gemini vision proof via two different images). Regression suite 15/16 pass; the only failure is voice TTS returning a transient 502 from the upstream provider (unrelated to this iteration). Frontend: landing hero shows 'Patient safety, worth a second pair of eyes' and contains NO hardcoded marketing numbers (62/100, 7 medicines, 94% adherence not present). Authenticated /app/caregiver, /app/pill-check, /app/prescriptions all render; UI invite creation produces Copy/Revoke buttons; rx-pdf-btn downloads a real ~3.2 KB PDF; public /cg/{token} renders patient name, RiskGauge, adherence and active medicines with NO auth cookie.

minor.issue: Transient 502 from upstream TTS provider (HTML CF error page). Pre-existing dependency; not introduced by this iteration. Retry usually succeeds.

issues: Adherence card uses label 'ADHERENCE' (uppercase) — works but unusual; ensure the score number color contrasts on light theme.

action_items: (Optional) Add data-testid='rx-card-{id}' on each prescription list item in Prescriptions.jsx so automated tests don't have to fall back to text selectors.
(Optional) Add retry/backoff or graceful degradation around /api/voice/tts when the upstream returns 5xx — currently surfaces a raw 500 with HTML body.
Server.py is now >1080 lines — strongly consider splitting routers (caregiver/pill/reports into their own modules) per code-review threshold.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew from 818 → 1085 lines; routes for caregiver/pill/reports should move into separate APIRouters.
public_caregiver_view recomputes adherence inline (duplicates logic from /api/adherence/summary). Extract a private helper that takes a user_id to avoid drift.
caregiver_invite token uses new_id('cgt') + uuid4().hex[:12] (~22+12 chars). Acceptable, but consider secrets.token_urlsafe(24) for uniformity and cryptographic strength.
/api/reports/{prescription_id}/pdf is synchronous reportlab inside an async handler — for large prescriptions this can block the event loop briefly. Wrap _build_pdf in asyncio.to_thread.
Pill check writes upload bytes to a tempfile then deletes — fine, but no per-user rate-limit; combined with Gemini cost, add a simple in-memory throttle.
DELETE /api/caregiver/invite/{token} returns ok:true even when no document matched (idempotent but silently hides typos). Consider 404 when matched_count==0.

success_rate.backend: 100% on iteration-2 (9/9); 93.75% regression (15/16) — TTS 502 is upstream transient

test_credentials: session_token=test_session_mediguard_v1 (Bearer + cookie) for user_id=user_test_mediguard. For headless browsers, override UA to a real Chrome string to bypass Cloudflare HeadlessChrome filtering.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert one expired caregiver invite directly in mongo (cleaned up). Each pill-check test issues 1-2 real Gemini-Vision calls; caregiver invites created during tests are revoked/deleted on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration2_features.py -v` for new-feature regression and `pytest backend/tests/test_mediguard_backend.py -v` for full regression. Frontend playwright MUST set UA to a real Chrome string and set cookie session_token=test_session_mediguard_v1 on domain medication-intel.preview.emergentagent.com (secure, sameSite=None) — otherwise Cloudflare blocks /api fetches and the SPA hangs on 'Loading...'. Public route /cg/{token} requires no cookie. PDF button only appears after clicking a prescription card — cards currently lack a stable data-testid (use text='Dr Sharma' fallback).
rca_of_the_issue: None for this iteration. Voice TTS 502 traced to upstream provider's CF edge returning HTML — pre-existing, not caused by this iteration's changes.
